01
What it actually is
What It Is

A PanderThing is a zipped HTML document — self-enclosed, clicky-movey — that you email to people as an attachment. They unzip it, click the file, and it opens in their browser. No internet needed. No algorithm between you and them.

Party in front.
Business in back.

The front is simple: an intro, a summary, a custom pitch. When people click what interests them, the back appears — more text, more images, more of exactly that one thing. If you’re selling pie and ice cream Saturday: they click the pie picture and see everything that makes them want that pie. Then they come in. Dee-lish.

Most PanderThings have two levels. Simple. Not a website. Not a newsletter. Something that does what neither of those does quite right.

Style: black on white, or white on black, or a mix. Your photos and logo colours really pop.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Open Hearts, Open Hands

Started 2016 with 25 people fed. Now over 400 families, Logan County and beyond. Non-denominational. Led by board chairman Tammy Heltsley. Nobody’s getting paid.

A faith-based food ministry, Lewisburg, KY  ·  Founded 2016  ·  openheartsopenhandsinc.org

02
Simple as that
How It Works

You give us one to twelve things you want people to know about, with good photos. Real photos of your place, your food, your people. We prefer real over stock. Real has aura. Stock does not.

You give us the stuff.
We make the thing.
You approve it.
You mail it out.

Put each photo in a word document. Under each photo, write a description between double brackets like this: [[This is the front of our shop on a Tuesday. The sign was made by my brother-in-law.]] That’s it. We do the rest.

Note: videos are not included in standard PanderThings. A PanderThing is a self-contained document — no links out, no third parties, no tracking. That’s part of the promise.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Open Hearts, Open Hands

Partners with Hunters for the Hungry — nearly 1,000 lb of deer meat to families at the holidays. In season: venison on the table. That’s Logan County looking after Logan County.

A faith-based food ministry, Lewisburg, KY  ·  Founded 2016  ·  openheartsopenhandsinc.org

03
For properly skeptical folk
For Skeptics

Every PanderThing email also includes a PDF with the same information — all the text and images, in a plain document requiring no unzipping, containing no macros, incapable of doing anything to your computer except being read.

People suspicious about
zip attachments are right.
We say so in the file name.

We call this the PanderThing Alternative File for Properly Skeptical Folk. That is its actual name. The suspicion is healthy. When your customers see you validating their caution, you look honest. Which you are. That’s worth something.

Technical note: the HTML uses JavaScript for its effects. It is entirely self-contained. It does not phone home. It does not collect data. It does not place cookies. It is a document, not a trap. But the skeptics are still right to ask.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Open Hearts, Open Hands

Every distribution, volunteers pray with the people receiving food. The cars circle past the campers where Lewisburg Looks was born. It is a great blessing to see.

A faith-based food ministry, Lewisburg, KY  ·  Founded 2016  ·  openheartsopenhandsinc.org

04
A little loco
Locomprotion

When you order a PanderThing, we strongly encourage you to put in a good word for another Logan County ma and pop business — or something in the area that’s good for the soul but not getting the attention it deserves.

Say something nice
about a competitor.

“The other day we went to Harry’s BBQ — AND — OH HEAVEN LICK MY TOES!! Their FRIES, their fries their fries!! You haven’t LIVED until you’ve tasted Harry’s fries!!”

This looks really good for both of you. It looks real because it is real. People can smell the difference. And it gets folks talkin.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Open Hearts, Open Hands

Young people sort, pack, and hand out food alongside their elders. This is what a community looks like when it decides to actually be one.

A faith-based food ministry, Lewisburg, KY  ·  Founded 2016  ·  openheartsopenhandsinc.org

★
Worth knowing about
The Star in the P

Look at the logo. The hole in the P — the counter — is not a circle. It is a star.

The desire at the centre
of every PanderThing:
to be a star.

That desire is honest. Most promotion is driven by exactly this. The business wants to be known. The person wants to be seen. The product wants to matter. Nothing wrong with that. It is even, in certain lights, beautiful.

But naming it changes something. When the star is visible — right there in the logo, beaming — it becomes something to examine rather than something to be driven by without knowing it.

The star is not the problem. Forgetting it’s there is the problem.

The locomprotion — the good word for the competitor, the Open Hearts volunteers in their yellow vests, the iron mountain honey — that is what the star-desire is for.

★  OUR LOCOMPROTION  ★
Open Hearts, Open Hands

2025: new satellite location at Dunmor Missionary Baptist Church. Growing without outside large-scale support — buying food directly, trusting local donations. 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

A faith-based food ministry, Lewisburg, KY  ·  Founded 2016  ·  openheartsopenhandsinc.org
